Read right-to-left, all the census columns to the RIGHT of the ○marker belong to the PREVIOUS domain, Yunagaya (湯長谷藩, a small ~15,000-koku Iwaki domain whose entry began on the prior leaf): 46 temples, 5 gyōya, 115 outcaste houses, a total registered population of 7,766 (male 3,931 / female 3,835), 38 shrine-priests, 6 shugendō ascetics, 29 monks, 5 nobility (male 2 / female 3), 6 shrines, and 72 soldiers (the last flagged with an editorial (原朱) 'originally in vermilion' note). MASTER captured only Mito's 350,000-koku figure and mis-assigned the whole page to Mito, missing the entire Yunagaya census block on the right.

pages.page_topic: Mito domain assessed koku (kokudaka), land totals, and tax receipts split between Hitachi and Shimotsuke provinces
pages.page_summary_en: This is the left half of physical page 115 of Fascicle 7 of the Hansei Ichiran (1870), covering Mito domain (水戸藩), confirmed by the outer-margin running header 藩制一覧表第七 （水戸）. The columns record fiscal and land-assessment figures: the current assessed yield (當高) of the Hitachi (常陸) main holding at 325,606.037 koku including new fields, of which 20,000 koku is administered by the sub-fief steward Nakayama Bitchū-no-kami; the Shimotsuke (下野) portion at 18,652.845 koku; a combined figure of 297,875.297 koku; new-field additions of 9,077.895 koku; and an aggregate holding (合高) of 306,953.192 koku. The right portion also lists the true land rice (正租米) of 42,859.992 koku and true land money (正租金) of 28,297 ryō 3 bu; the left group under the heading 雜税納高 (miscellaneous tax paid) breaks out millet (稗) 3,148.06 koku plus separate money and rice sums for Hitachi and Shimotsuke. Several MASTER values were misread and are corrected here (當高 325,606 not 325,663; 合高 306,953 not 306,775; 高 297,875.297 not .295; 正租金 tail 819 mon not 890; 金 下野 10,090 ryō written 一萬九十 not 一萬九千八十二), and the missed 當高 18,652.845 Shimotsuke column is added.

pages.page_topic: Hikone domain land assessment, tax revenue (rice, gold, copper-cash), and totals
pages.page_summary_en: This right-hand half-page opens the Hikone (彦根藩) entry in the seventh fascicle (第七) of the Hansei Ichiran, marked by the ○ domain-boundary marker before ○彦根藩 with a subscript giving the base assessment (本高) of 200,000 koku. It records the domain's fiscal register: total assessed koku (惣高 = 203,887.99 koku, including new-field re-surveys), regular land tax (正租) in rice and gold, miscellaneous taxes (雜税) in rice, gold, and copper-cash, and a bracketed grand total (合計) in rice, gold, and cash, closing with the current-rice figure (現米 = 94,340.03 koku). A marginal note (以下十行原書欄外・辛未正月調) records that the following ten lines were entered in the original document's margin, surveyed in the first month of the metal-sheep year (1871); the entry also states the governor's family was not recorded (知事家族不記).

pages.page_topic: Hikone domain fiscal register (fascicle 7): continuation of the tax-revenue itemization (內譯), giving the principal land-tax rice and cash, miscellaneous-tax rice and cash, cash coin (錢), and a supplementary block for shrine/temple vermillion-seal land (社寺領朱印地) in Ōmi, Musashi and Shimotsuke provinces.
pages.page_summary_en: This left half-page continues the fascicle-7 fiscal breakdown for Hikone domain (彦根藩), headed 內譯 ("itemized breakdown"). The main columns record the principal land tax in rice (正租米 83,216.42642 koku) and cash (金 2,248 ryō), the miscellaneous tax in rice (雜稅米 20,562.823156 koku) and cash (金 348 ryō), and coin cash (錢 9,319 kan 97 mon). A separate block for shrine- and temple-held vermillion-seal land (社寺領朱印地) spread across Ōmi, Musashi and Shimotsuke provinces gives its assessed yield (高 1,660.10575 koku), its rice equivalent (此米 441 koku), its cash equivalent (此金 931 ryō), and a marginal note on the actual yield (物成 729.21652 koku) plus an "additionally" note of forest in three places and 2 tan of dry field. The bottom-margin printed page number is 173. Three MASTER values were corrected against the scan: 正租米 (83216 not 83026), 雜稅米 sub-koku (八斗貳升三合… not 八斗四升三合), and 錢 (九千三百十九貫九十七文 not 九千三百九貫九百九十七文).

pages.page_topic: Tail of Hikone's fiscal register: miscellaneous produce/grain taxes (barley, soybean, hulled rice, millet), the copper-cash and eiraku-money miscellaneous levy, plus a marginal shrine/temple-land (社寺領) supplementary listing with its combined-provinces kusadaka and a note that the yield equals the main-text total.
pages.page_summary_en: This right half-page continues and closes the Hikone (彦根藩) entry in Hansei Ichiran fascicle 7 (藩制一覧表第七), a civil/fiscal register; the outer-margin running header names Hikone and the bottom margin gives printed book page 百七十四 (174). Reading right to left, the main columns record the domain's miscellaneous produce and grain taxes — an eiraku-money miscellaneous levy (永八十四貫四百三十六文六分八厘九毛), hulled rice (籾), millet (稗), soybean (大豆), barley (麥), and copper cash (錢三十三貫九百七十九文). A parenthetical note (以下七行原書欄外 別紙社寺領調) flags that the following seven lines were written in the margin of the original as a separate shrine-and-temple-land survey; these give a combined-three-provinces (Ōmi/Musashi/[Shimo]tsuke) kusadaka of 千六百六十石余, plus small upland-field, forest, and yield-reconciliation entries. All numeric values transcribed here confirm the MASTER extraction with no corrections needed.

pages.page_topic: Hikone domain household and population register: total households, samurai/soldier/temple/shrine/outcaste household counts, and total population with status and male/female breakdowns.
pages.page_summary_en: This is the demographic register (fascicle 7) for Hikone domain (彦根藩), the Ii family's fief in Ōmi. The right-hand block records household counts: a total of 42,145 households (戸數), broken down into upper-rank samurai (817), lower-rank samurai / retainers (434), soldier-class households (2,707), temples (498 buildings, with a note on 46 vacant, 224 practice halls, and 11 Honganji branch stations), Shinto priest households (115), outcaste 非人 households (48), and 穢多 households (897). The left-hand block records the total population of 177,518 persons (88,212 male / 89,306 female), further itemized by status: upper samurai 5,066, lower samurai 1,542, soldier-class 6,494, Shinto priests 222, Buddhist clergy 2,129 (of whom 1,110 monks and 12 nuns), and 非人 189. Every value cross-checks against the MASTER extraction; the household breakdown pairs 卒族/社家 and 非人/穢多 vertically within shared columns.

pages.page_topic: Civil register (fascicle 7): tail of Hitotsubashi domain's temple/shrine/village counts, then the ○Morioka boundary marker and Morioka's kokudaka.
pages.page_summary_en: This civil-register half-page is a domain boundary. Read right-to-left, the columns from the running header down through 軍艦運輸舶ナシ and 知事家族記セス are the demographic/religious/administrative tail of Hitotsubashi domain (一橋藩) — the entry that runs off the preceding pages — including its Shinto priests (M163/F148), 452 temples (+3 on tax-exempt land), 703 shrines, 320 villages, 123 halls/hermitages, and a note that the governor's family is unrecorded and there are no warships. The ○盛岡藩 maru-marker then opens the Morioka (Nanbu) entry, whose only datum on this page is its kokudaka of 130,000 koku (拾三萬石) printed to the marker's left; Morioka's fiscal and population figures continue on the following pages. MASTER's page-mapping keyed this image to Morioka, but the bulk of the data belongs to Hitotsubashi; MASTER's own field rows already attribute the right-block correctly to 一橋藩. The printed book page number in the bottom margin is 二百 (200).

pages.page_topic: Morioka domain kokudaka, land-tax and miscellaneous-tax revenue (rice, soybeans, gold, cash), and product taxes (hinoki boards, charcoal).
pages.page_summary_en: This is the left half of a Fascicle-7 (civil/fiscal) opening for Morioka domain (盛岡藩), running header 藩制一覧表第七（盛岡）, book page 201. There is no ○ domain-marker on this half-page: it is a continuation of Morioka's own fiscal register, so every column belongs to Morioka. The columns record the combined assessed kokudaka (合高 160,923.932 koku) with its internal deduction (内 516.286 koku written off for flood/waste land) and an external newly-reclaimed increment (外 30,923.932 koku, 新田改出), then the main land tax (正租) broken into rice 69,927.04 koku, soybeans 1,623.93 koku, gold 6,660兩3分, and cash 23,633貫300文, followed by the miscellaneous tax (雜稅) in gold 6,817兩1分 and cash 38,605貫600文, and product taxes in hinoki boards (檜柾 180,000 sheets) and charcoal (炭 420 bales); a 總戸數 (total households) header appears with its value carried on the facing half-page. A small marginal note explains that for the Tora and Tatsu years (寅辰), owing to crop failure, the ordinary fixed-exemption five-year average was applied. Two MASTER misreads were corrected here (雜稅金 6818→6817; 正租-cash 23632→23633) and the previously-missing 雜稅-cash column (38,605貫600文) was added.
